subject: Ecology
title: Dispersal by Wind
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Dispersal by Wind.

hook.text: Tiny spiders, fern spores, and seeds ride wind currents across hundreds of kilometers of ocean.

buildup.text: Spiders balloon on silk threads, catching updrafts that carry them to distant islands.

discovery.text: Fern spores are so small that storms can loft them into the jet stream across entire oceans.

twist.text: Wind dispersal is random—millions launch, but only a few land somewhere they can survive.

climax.text: Wind-dispersed species often colonize new volcanic islands within years of eruption.

punchline.text: Wind is the oldest airline. No ticket needed.

quiz.question: How do spiders disperse to remote islands?
options: They balloon on silk threads carried by wind
